things.name: Guimarães Castle
things.description: Birthplace of Portugal's founding king Afonso Henriques in 1109, a magnificent medieval keep with towers and ramparts to explore

things.name: Palace of the Dukes of Braganza
things.description: Imposing 15th-century ducal palace fully restored to its medieval glory with tapestries, armour, porcelain and royal portraits

things.name: Historic Centre of Guimarães (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage medieval city centre of exceptional integrity, the cradle of Portuguese nationhood and identity

things.name: Largo da Oliveira
things.description: Magnificent medieval square with the Gothic Padrão do Salado monument and the Romanesque collegiate church of Nossa Senhora da Oliveira

things.name: CIAJG Contemporary Art Centre
things.description: Striking contemporary arts centre by Pritzker Prize architect Álvaro Siza Vieira, presenting international exhibitions

things.name: Monte Latito Cable Car
things.description: Scenic cable car ascending to Monte Latito sanctuary with panoramic views over Guimarães old town and the Minho hills

things.name: Santa Marinha da Costa Monastery
things.description: Twelfth-century monastery converted into a magnificent pousada hotel, with cloisters, gardens and hilltop views over the city

things.name: Alberto Sampaio Museum
things.description: Sacred art museum in a medieval cloister displaying treasures including the tunic worn by King João I at the Battle of Aljubarrota

things.name: Rua de Santa Maria
things.description: Guimarães' most beautiful medieval street connecting the castle to the old town square, lined with arcaded stone buildings

things.name: Citânia de Briteiros
things.description: Remarkable hilltop Iron Age Celtic settlement with hundreds of circular stone house foundations visible in the Minho countryside
